mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating negative forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers immediate earnings recession fears, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with falling inflation indicates strong real consumer demand without monetary tightening pressure, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model with a Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over any 5-year out-of-sample period, or retail sales growth does not correlate with equity returns when inflation is stable.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, any positive month-over-month change in industrial production signals supply chain normalization and inventory restocking, generating positive short-term market returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of positive industrial production months during recovery periods, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect robust construction sector activity and consumer confidence, which consistently correlates with positive forward equity market performance.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.01 over a 4-year rolling window, or housing starts growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when mortgage rates exceed 7 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 310.0, persistent price pressures force expectations of prolonged restrictive monetary policy, triggering negative equity returns as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: The threshold signal generates positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered cases, or inflation above 310.0 fails to correlate with subsequent equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive quarterly returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over any 3-year sample, or quarterly returns remain flat despite strong labor data.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent signals an overheating economy that precedes central bank tightening and subsequent equity market corrections.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 70 percent of high-GDP quarters, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns when GDP growth is driven by fiscal stimulus rather than private demand.
